conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I have one child - my son Alex. He's 20 years old and lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. 

To assess Alex's eligibility, I need to understand his current situation a bit better. Is Alex currently in full-time non-advanced education or approved vocational training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in any education or training at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that.

Could you tell me - is Alex currently working, and what is he doing day-to-day?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't working at the moment. He's just been at home, taking some time to figure out what he wants to do next.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information.

Just to confirm a few final details - are you the person responsible for Alex (meaning you live with him and are responsible for his care), and are there any other special circumstances I should know about, such as:

- Does Alex have any disabilities or receive any disability-related benefits?
- Is there anything else about his situation that might be relevant?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm responsible for Alex - he lives with me at home. As for special circumstances, no, he doesn't have any disabilities or receive any benefits in his own name. There's nothing else really - just a normal situation.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have enough details to assess Alex's eligibility for Child Benefit.

Based on what you've told me, unfortunately **you would not be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**. 

The key issue is Alex's age. Child Benefit is generally only payable for children under 16 years old, or for young people aged 16-19 who are in full-time non-advanced education or approved vocational training. 

Since Alex is 20 years old and is not in any education or training, he falls outside the age criteria for Child Benefit eligibility.

Let me record this outcome for you.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Is there anything else I can help you with regarding Child Benefit or any other questions you might have?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 20 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 20, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 20, which is 20 or over
circumstances: Child is 20, which is 20 or over
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 20 years old and not in full-time non-advanced education or approved vocational training. Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or for young people aged 16-19 who are in qualifying education or training. Since Alex is 20 years old and not in education or training, he does not meet the age criteria for Child Benefit eligibility.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is responsible for one child, Alex, who is 20 years old and not in education or training. Alex exceeds the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility, which ends at 20th birthday or when turning 20, and only extends to age 19 for those in qualifying education or training. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 20 years old and not in full-time non-advanced education or approved vocational training. Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or for young people aged 16-19 who are in qualifying education or training. Since Alex is 20 years old and not in education or training, he does not meet the age criteria for Child Benefit eligibility.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is responsible for one child, Alex, who is 20 years old and not in education or training. Alex exceeds the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility, which ends at 20th birthday or when turning 20, and only extends to age 19 for those in qualifying education or training. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.
